L1.area: XML configuration parsing and recognition logic

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where an XML parser factory is instantiated without enabling secure processing features or disabling external entity resolution, allowing an attacker to include external entity references in crafted XML input that could lead to file disclosure or server-side request forgery.

L3.area: XML configuration root tag recognition and parsing subsystem
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where a SAX parser factory used for recognizing XML configuration root tags is instantiated without enabling secure processing features or disabling external entity resolution, allowing an attacker to include external entity references in crafted XML configuration input that could lead to file disclosure or server-side request forgery.

ground_truth.reason: The AbstractXmlConfigRootTagRecognizer constructor creates a SAXParserFactory without enabling XXE protection features, allowing attackers to craft XML input with external entity references that can read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery.
